MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT FATON BINAKU'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS OFF CALENDAR. THE CASE WAS DISMISSED. Judge: Harold E. Kahn Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR# 7077 =(301/HEK)

Ntc Of Motion To Compel Pltfs Response To Defts Inspection Demand And For Sanctions;
Off Calendar Jun-10-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCE
Steve Collier, Esq., appeared for defendant and moving party: Faton Binaku
No other appearance.

RULING:
TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendant Faton Binaku's Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Response To Defendant's Inspection Demand is moot as responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses on this motion. The court does not find that all privileges have been waived. If any documents have been withheld pursuant to a claim of privilege, they must be identified with particularity. The court finds that the late responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $320, to be paid within 20 days of notice of this order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Ntc And Motion For An Order Compellingpltfs Responses To Defts Special And Form Interrogatories And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-10-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCE
Steve Collier, Esq., appeared for defendant and moving party: Faton Binaku
No other appearance.

RULING:
TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendant's Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Response To Defendant's Special And Form Interrogatories is moot as responses have been served. The court finds that the late responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $320, to be paid within 20 days of notice of this order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED
